meta.title: Pasta Making in Calgary | Mixler
meta.description: Make fresh pasta from scratch in Calgary. Learn to mix, knead, roll, and shape your own pasta in a fun hands-on class. Eat what you make. All skill levels welcome.

content.subtitle: Mix, knead, and roll fresh pasta from scratch, then sit down and eat it.
content.intro: Pasta making classes in Calgary are one of the most satisfying food experiences you can have as a group. There's something primal about turning flour and eggs into a smooth, silky dough with your hands, then cutting and shaping it into something that actually tastes better than anything from a box. A chef guides you through every step, and the meal at the end is genuinely excellent.

what_to_expect.heading: The dough from scratch
what_to_expect.body: You'll mix and knead your own pasta dough by hand. The instructor teaches you what the texture should feel like at each stage.

what_to_expect.heading: Rolling and shaping
what_to_expect.body: Learn to roll the dough thin and shape it into fettuccine, tagliatelle, or another style depending on the class.

what_to_expect.heading: Sauce and finishing
what_to_expect.body: Many classes pair the pasta with a sauce you also make from scratch. It rounds out the whole meal.

what_to_expect.heading: Sit down and eat together
what_to_expect.body: You cook, then you eat. The meal at the end of a pasta class is always a highlight of the whole experience.

tips: Let your dough rest properly after kneading. Skipping the rest makes it tear and snap when you roll it.
Flour your surface generously. Pasta dough sticks quickly, especially when it's thin.
Fresh pasta cooks in 2 to 3 minutes. That's much faster than dried pasta, so watch it closely.
Pasta making is one of Calgary's most popular date night activities. It's hands-on, a little messy, and you share a real meal.
Mixler lists pasta making classes in Calgary when they come up. Join the waitlist and we'll send you a heads up.
content.waitlist_heading: Want to know when we run pasta making events?
content.waitlist_body: Join the waitlist and we'll email you when we add one. We use this to plan what to run next.

faq.q: Do I need to know how to cook?
faq.a: No. Pasta making classes are designed for complete beginners. The instructor walks you through everything.

faq.q: What type of pasta will I make?
faq.a: Most beginner classes focus on a long pasta like fettuccine or tagliatelle. Check the event listing for specifics.

faq.q: Do I eat the pasta I make?
faq.a: Yes. You cook and plate your pasta at the end of the class. It's a real meal.
Fresh pasta can be taken home and cooked within a day or two. Ask the instructor how to store it properly.
